name: Pan-Fried Noodles
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
This is a basic chow mein. You can add meat or vegetables as you like.
1. Blanch 1 pound of fresh noodles in boiling water for about 2 minutes to wash away the starch and loosen them up. Drain well.
2. Scatter the noodles in a baking pan. Use chopsticks and hands to untangle them as much as possible.
3. In two large, heavy skillets, lay a layer of noodles (1-inch deep). Add 2 tablespoons oil and 2/3 cup chicken broth.
4. Cook over low heat until the broth boils away and the bottom becomes brown and crisp. Occasionally add oil and broth to keep noodles moist and allow crust to develop slowly (10-12 minutes).
5. To flip, shake pan to ensure cake isn't stuck; if it is, dribble some oil around edge to loosen.
6. Slide skillet under wide lid, holding firmly. Flip skillet, releasing cake onto lid. Return cake to pan to brown other side.
7. Add more oil if needed to prevent sticking.
8. Serve on plate; use as base for any sauce-based dish.
TO MAKE THE PORK AND VEGETABLE SAUCE:
1. Heat peanut oil in a wok.
2. Add bok choy and stir-fry until coated with oil.
3. Add pork pieces and heat through.
4. Pour in broth, soy sauce, rice wine/sherry, and oyster sauce.
5. When sauce is hot, thicken with dissolved cornstarch. Serve over pan-fried noodles.
